campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on May 19, 2026, to discuss staffing vacancies and recruitment challenges for the city. The council also heard public comments on various matters and noted that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Public hearing under Government Code Section 3502
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council adopted a resolution to identify certain properties and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can voice concerns or objections before the city makes a final decision on the matter.
keyTopics: Property identification resolution approved by council
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests or comments at the hearing

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, appoint a new Youth Commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election for three open seats. The council also approved a new housing development map, renewed software agreements for permitting and building services, and authorized budget spending.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general election scheduled for three Town Council seats
New Youth Commissioner appointment confirmed by council
Tract 10675 Boulevard housing development map approved
Multi-year software agreement with Accela for permitting system
Building division plan check agreements and budget authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for June 16 and June 23, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to these meeting schedules and determined timing for various agenda items that need to be addressed.
keyTopics: Planning the June 16, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Planning the June 23, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Community and Economic Development Committee work plan

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met on June 9, 2026, to handle personnel matters behind closed doors and then celebrate two important observances with official proclamations. The council appointed a new City Attorney and publicly recognized Pride Month and Juneteenth.
keyTopics: City Attorney appointment (discussed in closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 16, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 16, 2026. The first was a closed session (private meeting) with no public details released, followed by a study session where council reviewed a Community Equity Assessment to evaluate fairness and inclusion across city services and programs.
keyTopics: Community Equity Assessment update and review
Closed session meeting
Study session format for policy discussion

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to ensure they comply with state law protections. The meeting also included a public comment period where residents could address the Council on any non-agenda items.
keyTopics: Review of current zoning designations for parks and recreational areas
Zoning corrections to align with state law requirements
Protection of parkland sites under state regulations
Public comment period for residents on other city matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting for the Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee to discuss issues affecting neighborhoods around the stadium. The city is accepting public input both in-person and online through Zoom.
keyTopics: Stadium neighborhood relations and community concerns
Public participation options (in-person, Zoom, and phone)
Written public comment submission process via email

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review updates to their federal housing and community development plan for 2025-2026, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and approve meeting minutes and schedules. Council members also submitted new work requests to be reviewed by staff.
keyTopics: Amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan for federal housing funding
New council workplan item requests from individual councilmembers
Proposed changes to the Council Vacancy Ordinance
Approval of meeting minutes and City Council calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council met to discuss updates from various departments and committees, review improvements planned for the Foothills Nature Preserve, and decide on the future management and direction of the middle school athletics program. The council also confirmed the Parks and Recreation Commission's work plan for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project – planning and updates
Middle School Athletics Program – deciding on management and program direction
Parks and Recreation Commission Fiscal Year 2027 Work Plan – review and confirmation
Department reports and staff presentations
Ad Hoc Committee and Liaison updates
